content.text: Deny Settings

Deployment Stack deny settings prevent unauthorized modifications to stack-managed resources using an Azure Policy deny assignment created by the stack service at assignment time. Three modes are available: None (no protection), DenyDelete (blocks deletions but allows modifications), and DenyWriteAndDelete (blocks all writes and deletions). The deny policy applies to all authenticated identities including Owner — only the stack service itself and explicitly excluded principals can bypass it. Deny settings are part of the az stack sub create or az stack sub set command; they are not separate Azure Policy assignments and cannot be removed without updating the stack.

attrs.alt: Deployment Stack deny setting modes, scope propagation behavior, and exclusion options

content.text: Deny settings differ from Azure Resource Manager locks: ARM locks are object-level locks applied to individual resources and can be removed by anyone with Owner on the resource. Deployment Stack deny settings are Policy-enforced at stack scope and require the stack service to modify — Owner access alone is insufficient to override them.
Deny Mode Reference
Mode
Blocks Writes
Blocks Deletes
Use Case
None
No
No
Dev/test environments where app teams must modify resources freely; stack tracks resources without governance enforcement
DenyDelete
No
Yes
Production landing zones — prevent accidental deletion while allowing config changes (VM scaling, storage tier changes, tag updates)
DenyWriteAndDelete
Yes
Yes
Compliance-frozen environments — configuration must exactly match the deployed template; use excluded actions for specific operations app teams must perform
Creating Stacks with Deny Settings

Deny settings are specified at stack creation and can be changed via az stack sub set without redeploying resources. Use --deny-settings-apply-to-child-scopes on all production stacks — this ensures resources added to managed resource groups after stack creation also inherit the deny protection. Without this flag, only resources explicitly listed in the stack's managed resource set are protected. Specify --deny-settings-excluded-principals with the object IDs of break-glass accounts and pipeline service principals that must bypass the deny to update managed resources.

# Create and update Deployment Stacks with deny settings

TARGET_SUB='<landing-zone-sub-id>'
BREAK_GLASS_OID='<break-glass-account-oid>'
PIPELINE_SP_OID='<pipeline-service-principal-oid>'

# Create stack with DenyDelete (recommended for most production stacks)
az stack sub create \
  --name 'landing-zone-baseline' \
  --location eastus \
  --subscription $TARGET_SUB \
  --template-file landing_zone_baseline.bicep \
  --parameters @landing_zone_baseline.parameters.json \
  --deny-settings-mode DenyDelete \
  --deny-settings-apply-to-child-scopes \
  --deny-settings-excluded-principals $BREAK_GLASS_OID \
  --action-on-unmanage detachAll

# Create stack with DenyWriteAndDelete for compliance-frozen environment
# Exclude pipeline SP so CI/CD can still update via az stack sub set
az stack sub create \
  --name 'compliance-baseline' \
  --location eastus \
  --subscription $TARGET_SUB \
  --template-file compliance_baseline.bicep \
  --parameters @compliance_baseline.parameters.json \
  --deny-settings-mode DenyWriteAndDelete \
  --deny-settings-apply-to-child-scopes \
  --deny-settings-excluded-principals $PIPELINE_SP_OID $BREAK_GLASS_OID \
  --action-on-unmanage detachAll

# Show current deny settings on a stack
az stack sub show \
  --name 'landing-zone-baseline' \
  --subscription $TARGET_SUB \
  --query 'denySettings' -o json

# Update deny settings without changing the template
az stack sub set \
  --name 'landing-zone-baseline' \
  --subscription $TARGET_SUB \
  --template-file landing_zone_baseline.bicep \
  --parameters @landing_zone_baseline.parameters.json \
  --deny-settings-mode DenyWriteAndDelete \
  --deny-settings-apply-to-child-scopes \
  --deny-settings-excluded-principals $BREAK_GLASS_OID \
  --action-on-unmanage detachAll
Excluded Actions

When DenyWriteAndDelete is too broad, use --deny-settings-excluded-actions to exempt specific ARM action strings from the deny. This is narrower than excluding a principal (which bypasses the deny entirely) — excluded actions allow specific operations while keeping all other operations blocked. Common excluded actions in landing zone stacks: Key Vault secrets writes (application teams write their own secrets), diagnostic settings writes (application teams configure their own resource diagnostics), and Azure Monitor alert rule writes (application teams create their own alerts without modifying stack-managed infrastructure).

# Configure excluded actions for fine-grained deny bypass
# Use when DenyWriteAndDelete is too broad for a specific operation.

TARGET_SUB='<landing-zone-sub-id>'

# Scenario: DenyWriteAndDelete stack, but app team needs to write Key Vault secrets
# Without exclusion: az keyvault secret set fails with RequestDisallowedByPolicy
# With exclusion: only the secrets/write action is bypassed
az stack sub set \
  --name 'landing-zone-baseline' \
  --subscription $TARGET_SUB \
  --template-file landing_zone_baseline.bicep \
  --parameters @landing_zone_baseline.parameters.json \
  --deny-settings-mode DenyWriteAndDelete \
  --deny-settings-apply-to-child-scopes \
  --deny-settings-excluded-actions \
    'Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/secrets/write' \
    'Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/secrets/delete' \
  --action-on-unmanage detachAll

# Verify the exclusion is in place
az stack sub show \
  --name 'landing-zone-baseline' \
  --subscription $TARGET_SUB \
  --query 'denySettings.excludedActions' -o json

# Test that the exclusion works:
# This should succeed even with DenyWriteAndDelete on the stack
az keyvault secret set \
  --vault-name 'kv-<unique>' \
  --name 'test-secret' \
  --value 'test-value' \
  --subscription $TARGET_SUB

# Common excluded actions for landing zone stacks:
# Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/secrets/write          # App teams write secrets
# Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices/containers/blobs/write  # App data
# Microsoft.Insights/diagnosticSettings/write      # App teams configure own diag
# Microsoft.Authorization/locks/write              # Allow apps to lock their own resources
Testing Deny Settings

After creating a stack with deny settings, verify the protection is active by attempting a blocked operation — the error code should be RequestDisallowedByPolicy. Test the excluded principal path by logging in as a break-glass account and attempting the same blocked operation — it should succeed. Also verify that az stack sub set succeeds even under DenyWriteAndDelete — the stack service itself is exempt from its own deny assignments, so stack updates always work regardless of deny mode.

# Test deny settings behavior after stack creation

TARGET_SUB='<landing-zone-sub-id>'

# Test 1: Verify delete is blocked (DenyDelete mode)
# Attempt to delete a managed resource group — should fail
az group delete \
  --name 'rg-workloads-prod-eastus' \
  --yes \
  --subscription $TARGET_SUB 2>&1 || \
  echo 'Delete correctly blocked by denySettings'
# Expected: (AuthorizationFailed) The client ... does not have authorization to perform
# action 'Microsoft.Resources/resourcegroups/delete' ... disallowedByPolicy

# Test 2: For DenyWriteAndDelete — verify update is also blocked
az group update \
  --name 'rg-workloads-prod-eastus' \
  --tags TestTag=value \
  --subscription $TARGET_SUB 2>&1 || \
  echo 'Write correctly blocked by denySettings'

# Test 3: Verify excluded principal CAN delete (break-glass test)
# Must run this as the excluded break-glass account
# az login --tenant '<tenant-id>'  # login as break-glass
# az group delete --name 'rg-workloads-prod-eastus' --yes  # should succeed

# Test 4: Verify stack update (az stack sub set) works despite DenyWriteAndDelete
# The stack service itself bypasses deny settings during az stack sub set
az stack sub set \
  --name 'landing-zone-baseline' \
  --subscription $TARGET_SUB \
  --template-file landing_zone_baseline.bicep \
  --parameters @landing_zone_baseline.parameters.json \
  --deny-settings-mode DenyWriteAndDelete \
  --deny-settings-apply-to-child-scopes \
  --action-on-unmanage detachAll
# Stack operations always succeed regardless of deny mode —
# the stack deployment service is exempt from its own deny assignments.
Auditing Deny Events

Three KQL queries in Log Analytics surface deny setting activity: all operations blocked by stack deny (to detect users who need excluded-actions or excluded-principals), stack create/update events (to audit who changed deny settings), and the top users triggering deny events (to identify teams that need education or action exclusions). This data requires Activity Log connected to the central Log Analytics workspace via a diagnostic setting at the alz MG scope.

// KQL: Activity Log queries for Deployment Stack deny setting events

// 1. Denied operations on stack-managed resources (RequestDisallowedByPolicy from stack deny)
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ActivityStatusValue =~ 'Failed'
| where Properties contains 'disallowedByPolicy'
| extend
    Caller = tostring(parse_json(Authorization).principalId),
    Operation = OperationNameValue,
    Resource = _ResourceId,
    ErrorCode = tostring(parse_json(Properties).statusCode)
| where ErrorCode =~ 'RequestDisallowedByPolicy'
| project TimeGenerated, Caller, Operation, Resource, SubscriptionId
| order by TimeGenerated desc

// 2. Stack create/update events (who changed deny settings)
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where ResourceProvider =~ 'microsoft.resources'
| where OperationNameValue contains 'deploymentStacks'
| project TimeGenerated,
    Caller = tostring(parse_json(Authorization).principalId),
    Operation = OperationNameValue,
    StackName = Resource,
    Status = ActivityStatusValue
| order by TimeGenerated desc

// 3. Top users triggering deny events on stack-managed resources
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where tostring(parse_json(Properties).statusCode) =~ 'RequestDisallowedByPolicy'
| extend Caller = tostring(parse_json(Authorization).principalId)
| summarize DeniedCount = count() by Caller, OperationNameValue
| order by DeniedCount desc
| take 20
Common Deny Settings Mistakes
Mistake
Consequence
Fix
Using DenyWriteAndDelete without excluded principals on a CI/CD-managed subscription
The deployment pipeline SP is blocked from writing to managed resources — all pipeline runs fail with RequestDisallowedByPolicy
Add the pipeline SP OID as an excluded principal when creating or updating the stack
Not setting --deny-settings-apply-to-child-scopes
Resources added to managed resource groups by application teams after stack creation are not protected by the deny — they can be deleted without restriction
Always include --deny-settings-apply-to-child-scopes on production stacks
Confusing Deployment Stack deny with ARM locks
Removing an ARM lock does not remove stack deny protection — the stack deny is policy-based, not a resource lock
Stack deny settings require az stack sub set to modify; ARM lock removal has no effect on deny behavior
Excluding too many principals from deny
Excluded principals bypass deny entirely — a compromised excluded account can delete any managed resource
Limit exclusions to break-glass accounts (max 1-2); use excluded-actions for pipeline operations instead of excluded-principals
Setting DenyDelete at stack scope without apply-to-child
Resource group protection exists but new resources inside the RG added post-creation are unprotected
Rebuild the stack with --deny-settings-apply-to-child-scopes; this is a non-breaking update
Removing deny settings to fix a blocked operation instead of using excluded-actions
Removal of deny settings leaves all stack-managed resources unprotected while the fix is applied
Use az stack sub set to add the blocked action to excluded-actions rather than removing the deny mode entirely
Management-Group-Scoped Stacks

Deployment Stacks can be scoped to a management group (az stack mg create) to protect platform governance resources — custom policy definitions, policy initiatives, and MG-level role assignments — that span all subscriptions in the MG hierarchy. MG-scoped stacks are useful for platform teams managing ALZ policy baseline: the stack deny prevents unauthorized deletion of policy assignments, policy definitions, and role assignments at the alz MG scope. The deny applies to all subscriptions in the MG's hierarchy when --deny-settings-apply-to-child-scopes is used.
Deny Settings in CI/CD Pipelines

In CI/CD pipelines, parameterize the deny mode by environment — DenyDelete for production, None for development. This prevents the dev environment's loose governance from accidentally being promoted to production, while avoiding friction in dev where developers iterate quickly. The pipeline service principal must be in --deny-settings-excluded-principals for environments with DenyDelete or DenyWriteAndDelete so that az stack sub set can update managed resources during deployments. Use different excluded principal lists per environment to minimize blast radius.

# Define deny settings in Bicep (for Infrastructure as Code of the stack itself)
# When deploying stacks via CI/CD pipeline, parameterize deny mode and exclusions.

# Parameters file for different environments:
cat << 'EOF' > stack_params_prod.json
{
  "$schema": "https://schema.management.azure.com/schemas/2019-04-01/deploymentParameters.json#",
  "contentVersion": "1.0.0.0",
  "parameters": {
    "subscriptionLocation": {"value": "eastus"},
    "appTeamGroupObjectId": {"value": "<prod-app-team-oid>"},
    "logAnalyticsWorkspaceId": {"value": "/subscriptions/<mgmt>/..."} 
  }
}
EOF

cat << 'EOF' > stack_params_dev.json
{
  "$schema": "https://schema.management.azure.com/schemas/2019-04-01/deploymentParameters.json#",
  "contentVersion": "1.0.0.0",
  "parameters": {
    "subscriptionLocation": {"value": "eastus"},
    "appTeamGroupObjectId": {"value": "<dev-app-team-oid>"},
    "logAnalyticsWorkspaceId": {"value": "/subscriptions/<mgmt>/..."}
  }
}
EOF

# GitHub Actions: deploy with environment-appropriate deny settings
# Production: DenyDelete; Dev: None
cat << 'YAML'
jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        include:
          - env: prod
            sub: ${{ secrets.PROD_SUB_ID }}
            deny_mode: DenyDelete
            params: stack_params_prod.json
          - env: dev
            sub: ${{ secrets.DEV_SUB_ID }}
            deny_mode: None
            params: stack_params_dev.json
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: azure/login@v2
        with:
          client-id: ${{ secrets.AZURE_CLIENT_ID }}
          tenant-id: ${{ secrets.AZURE_TENANT_ID }}
          subscription-id: ${{ matrix.sub }}
      - name: Deploy Stack
        run: |
          az stack sub ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && 'set' || 'create' }} \
            --name landing-zone-baseline \
            --location eastus \
            --subscription ${{ matrix.sub }} \
            --template-file landing_zone_baseline.bicep \
            --parameters @${{ matrix.params }} \
            --deny-settings-mode ${{ matrix.deny_mode }} \
            --deny-settings-apply-to-child-scopes \
            --action-on-unmanage detachAll
YAML
Summary

Deployment Stack deny settings protect managed resources using Policy deny assignments: None (no protection), DenyDelete (blocks deletes, allows writes), DenyWriteAndDelete (blocks all writes and deletes). Use --deny-settings-apply-to-child-scopes on all production stacks to protect resources added after stack creation. Excluded principals bypass the deny entirely (use sparingly for break-glass accounts); excluded actions exempt specific ARM operations from the deny (use for pipeline and application team operations). Deny settings are enforced at Policy layer — Owner access alone cannot override them. The stack service itself is always exempt from its own deny assignments.
